name: From Rubió to the mountain of Sant Miquel — circular
name_original: De Rubió a la montaña de Sant Miquel — circular
region_intro: Jogging routes around Rubió are primarily defined by the Serra de Rubió mountain range in Catalonia, offering a diverse natural environment. The landscape features extensive forests of Aleppo Pine, Austrian Pine, Scots Pine, oaks, and holm oaks, providing varied terrain for runners. River valleys, such as those of the Maçana and Rubió rivers, offer contrasting flatter sections. This region provides a tranquil setting for outdoor activities, away from urban areas. Best jogging routes around Rubió The most popular jogging route is From Rubió to the mountain of Sant Miquel — circular, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 25 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Circular de la Serra de Rubió i Morrocurt, a difficult 11.3 miles (18.2 km) path. This route traverses the Serra de Rubió, offering expansive views and challenging ascents. Local runners also love the Riera de Rubió and Jorba Lock Loop, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail leading through river valleys and natural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 41 minutes. Jogging around Rubió is defined by mountainous terrain, dense pine forests, and serene river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest paths to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Rubió are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 1500 runners have used komoot to explore Rubió's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Rubió?
A: Rubió offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 160 trails documented on komoot. These range from easy paths to more challenging mountain runs, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Rubió?
A: Yes, Rubió has several options suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier run. There are 12 routes classified as easy, often winding through the serene pine forests and offering a gentle introduction to the region's natural beauty.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Rubió area?
A: Absolutely. Many runners prefer circular routes for convenience. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113692934/from-rubio-to-the-mountain-of-sant-miquel-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Rubió to the mountain of Sant Miquel — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.7 miles (10.8 km) long and offers significant elevation gain, providing a rewarding loop experience.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while running in Rubió?
A: The running routes in Rubió traverse diverse landscapes, primarily defined by the Serra de Rubió mountain range. You'll encounter extensive forests of Aleppo, Austrian, and Scots Pines, as well as oaks and holm oaks. River valleys, such as those of the Maçana and Rubió rivers, offer contrasting flatter sections, providing a varied and scenic experience.

Q: Are there any routes with good viewpoints or scenic spots?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of Rubió offers several opportunities for scenic views. You can find stunning vistas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5694171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Planes&lt;/a&gt; or even distant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Montserrat&lt;/a&gt; along some trails, providing breathtaking backdrops for your run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Rubió?
A: The running trails in Rubió are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Runners often praise the tranquility of the area, the diverse natural environment, and the well-maintained paths that allow for a peaceful escape into nature.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or natural attractions along the running paths?
A: Yes, Rubió's routes can lead you past interesting sites. You might discover natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3342696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cogulló of Can Torra&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2597745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alzina Grossa de Querol&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in history, there are also castles nearby, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5095529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Boixadors&lt;/a&gt;, which can be seen from some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Rubió?
A: Rubió offers pleasant running conditions for much of the year. The extensive pine forests provide shade during warmer months, while the varied terrain is enjoyable in spring and autumn. Even in winter, the trails can be beautiful, though checking local weather conditions is always advisable.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Experienced runners will find plenty of challenges in Rubió. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113728748/circular-de-la-serra-de-rubio-i-morrocurt" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular de la Serra de Rubió i Morrocurt&lt;/a&gt; is an 11.3 miles (18.2 km) difficult path that traverses the Serra de Rubió, offering expansive views and demanding ascents.

Q: Can I expect to see any wildlife while out on the trails?
A: Given Rubió's serene and natural environment, it's possible to encounter local wildlife during your runs. The area is home to various species, including roe deer, wild boar, red fox, European rabbit, and European hare, adding an extra dimension to your outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow river valleys?
A: Yes, the region features river valleys that offer different running experiences. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113739480/riera-de-rubio-and-jorba-lock-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Rubió and Jorba Lock Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that leads through river valleys and natural landscapes, providing a contrast to the mountainous paths.

Q: Are there any long-distance paths suitable for extended runs in Rubió?
A: The GR7 path, a well-known long-distance footpath, crosses the northern slope of the Serra de Rubió. This established route provides excellent opportunities for extended runs, allowing you to cover significant distances while enjoying the natural beauty of the pine forests and mountainous terrain.
tips: El Puig de Sant Miquel is a mountain of more than seven hundred meters in the Serra de Rubió, the same name as the nearby town. Going down the south-west face stands the church of Sant Pere d'Ardesa and from both points you can see beautiful views of the area.

Small town located above the Serra de Cererols. In Rubió there is the church of Santa Maria and also an old castle, which, thanks to its elevated geographical location, allowed you to see the surrounding lands.
